title: A shared playbook for trustworthy third party evaluations
optimized_headline: Unlocking Trust: A Collaborative Guide to Effective Third-Party Evaluations
raw_excerpt: OpenAI shares guidance on third-party AI evaluations, covering how to assess model capabilities, safeguards, and validity for frontier systems.
raw_body: OpenAI shares guidance on third-party AI evaluations, covering how to assess model capabilities, safeguards, and validity for frontier systems.
speedrun: OpenAI has released a guide for third-party evaluations of AI systems, focusing on how to assess their capabilities, safeguards, and overall validity. This playbook aims to standardize evaluations, especially for advanced AI models. It highlights the importance of thorough assessments to ensure reliability and trustworthiness. This matters now as the demand for transparent AI evaluation grows amidst increasing reliance on these technologies.
why_it_matters: This guidance helps developers and researchers ensure their AI systems are assessed accurately, fostering trust among users and stakeholders.
The move signals a broader shift toward accountability in AI, encouraging a culture of transparency and reliability in the tech industry.

lenses.eli12: OpenAI's new guide shows how to evaluate AI systems, focusing on their strengths and weaknesses. Think of it like a report card for AI, helping everyone understand how well these systems work. This is important for all of us because trustworthy AI can lead to better decisions in our daily lives.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this guide emphasizes the need for reliable assessments of AI capabilities. Understanding how to evaluate these systems can improve user trust and satisfaction. A practical implication is that incorporating these evaluation standards could enhance product development and marketing strategies.
lenses.engineer: The guide outlines methods for evaluating AI models, focusing on their capabilities and safeguards. It emphasizes the need for rigorous testing to validate performance, particularly for frontier systems. Engineers should consider these standards when designing and deploying AI to ensure accountability and reliability.
